objective: Analyser les changements non commités, déterminer leur feature d'appartenance, corriger les blocages, et rendre le travail livrable sans mélanger les concerns.

content: Working tree contains multiple workstreams; finish blockers first, then keep commit boundaries separate.
reason: Diff includes theme feature, E2E stabilization, lint/type cleanup, small bug fixes, and unrelated DAO ranking docs.

content: Fix hiway.connector.ts TJM null narrowing by extracting tjmMin to a local const before the filter closure
reason: TypeScript cannot narrow `context.tjmMin` (number|null) inside a .filter() closure because narrowing from the outer && condition does not carry into the callback scope. Extracting to a local const `tjmMin != null && tjmMin > 0` lets TS narrow correctly.

content: Add theme field to BackupDataSchema with .default('system') for backward compatibility with old backups
reason: AppSettings now includes theme, but BackupDataSchema.settings lacked it. Old backups without theme would fail validation or produce incomplete AppSettings on restore. Adding .default('system') ensures old backups parse correctly.

content: Merge restored backup settings with DEFAULT_SETTINGS in restoreBackup() for defense-in-depth
reason: Even though Zod schema now defaults theme to 'system', the restore path should also spread defaults to guard against future schema additions.

content: Backup schema now includes theme with default. Tests in tests/unit/backup/ already cover backward compat (V1 backup without theme field). All 1019 tests pass.
Created unit tests for backup theme preservation and setSettings theme validation. No E2E tests needed — theme is a pure state/persistence concern, not a multi-step UI flow.
reason: Chicago School TDD: state-based verification on pure backup/restore round-trip and Zod validation. The core backup module is in core/ (pure) and chrome-storage is shell (tested with minimal chrome mock).

content: No production code changes needed. Backup schema already fixed by @codegen (BackupDataSchema includes theme with .default('system')). All tests green.
reason: Verified @codegen fix is correct: Zod schema preserves theme in round-trip, backward compat with V1 backups without theme works via .default('system').

content: All theme tests green. No gaps found. The chrome-storage.test.ts setSettings reject test confirms invalid theme values are properly rejected at the Zod layer.

description: Test name claimed settings without theme should be rejected, but the assertion exercised the restore path where theme is explicitly re-added before saving.
resolution: Renamed the test to match actual behavior: restored settings are accepted once theme is supplied explicitly.

content: Keep finalization scope limited to theme/backup/typecheck coherence and exclude unrelated DAO docs and broader worktree changes from this integration slice.
reason: User requested finalization review only; the worktree contains multiple parallel workstreams with separate commit boundaries.

description: Renamed one storage test to match its actual setup and expectation.
DEFAULT_SETTINGS duplicated from shell/storage/chrome-storage.ts; candidate for extraction to core/types/app-settings.ts as DEFAULT_APP_SETTINGS

content: Scope is clean and coherent. tsc passes, 25/25 targeted tests pass. FC&IS preserved (no Core→Shell imports). Hiway null-narrowing fix is minimal and correct. Backup theme round-trip verified incl. V1 backward compat. Recommend APPROVED. Remaining ~50 modified files belong to ≥6 separate workstreams (lint cleanup, E2E stabilization, connector refactor, health, scan, docs/dao) and must NOT be bundled with this slice.

issues.description: DEFAULT_SETTINGS duplicated from shell/storage/chrome-storage.ts; extract to core/types/app-settings.ts as DEFAULT_APP_SETTINGS in a future cleanup commit
